level_signals: Explicitly described as 'senior individual contributor role'
Works directly with C-suite and senior executive stakeholders
'You'll own outcomes end-to-end and raise the operational ceiling of the company'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 15+ years of work experience, with a significant portion in operational roles at high-growth tech companies—product operations, strategy and operations, business operations, or company or corporate planning
A track record of building and owning operational systems. You have measurably improved how a company or large team operates, not just managed existing processes.
Deep experience either with company or functional planning—annual planning, OKR and SOKR-style goal-setting, resource allocation, and operating cadence design
A systems thinker who is automation-first—you instinctively reach for tools, data pipelines, and AI-augmented workflows to reduce manual overhead and increase leverage
Strong analytical rigor—you're comfortable owning data and metrics directly, running your own analyses, and pressure-testing assumptions without relying on a dedicated analytics partner
Experience working directly with and presenting to C-suite and senior executive stakeholders. You're comfortable owning the room and bringing a sharp, independent perspective.
High comfort with ambiguity—you take an undefined operational problem, scope it, design a solution, and drive it to completion with minimal oversight
Technical proficiency in AI tools in order to automate workflows as needed
Experience applying automation-thinking and scaled approaches to operational workflows—from analysis and synthesis to systems and decision support
Prior experience at a technology company is required.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in fintech, management consulting, or investment banking is a plus, but not required.
Experience with the product and infrastructure ecosystem at Stripe or similar complex platform businesses
Familiarity with modern planning tools, workflow automation platforms, and operational data infrastructure

scope.surface: Central Strategy and Operations — company-wide operational systems, planning processes, operating rhythms, and information architecture
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed and quality of leadership decision-making
Operational leverage and scalability of company-wide systems
core_responsibilities: Run planning for Stripe including annual planning workstreams and decision frameworks
Build, automate, and scale central operations infrastructure including data pipelines and AI-augmented workflows
Translate strategy into operational reality by preparing and running leadership-level operating forums
Drive continuous operational improvement and maintain a view of where operational systems are breaking down
Challenge assumptions and work across teams to address root causes of slow decisions, unclear handoffs, or diffuse accountability

green_flags: Clear ownership mandate — 'own outcomes end-to-end' with a well-defined operational surface
Senior IC role explicitly not a coordination or program management role — signals meaningful scope
Direct working relationships with most senior leaders at Stripe
Automation and AI-first orientation baked into role definition
Preferred qualifications are explicitly 'a bonus, not a requirement' — inclusive framing
red_flags: No location, remote policy, or compensation information disclosed in the JD
15+ years minimum is a high bar that excludes mid-career candidates
No visa sponsorship or work authorization guidance provided
Scope is broad and undefined ('building the infrastructure that makes Stripe faster') — may lead to role sprawl

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from '15+ years', 'senior individual contributor', and C-suite stakeholder access)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location or remote policy stated in JD
compensation.equity_mentioned set to false — no mention in JD
ambiguities: Location and remote eligibility are entirely absent from the JD body — it is unclear whether this role is onsite, hybrid, or remote, and in which city(ies)
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements are not mentioned anywhere in the JD
